title: crumbly resinous ethiopian bananas bale
description: a nostalgic ethiopian dish featuring bananas, kombu-cold-extract, and a crumbly texture that's perfect for a snack or dessert. inspired by the traditional ethiopian pastry known as bale

ingredients.name: kombu-cold-extract
ingredients.notes: see notes for kombu-cold-extract preparation method

instructions: mix together the flours, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
whisk together the coconut oil, honey, vanilla extract, and kombu-cold-extract in a separate bowl until well combined.
add the wet ingredients to the dry ingredients and mix until a crumbly dough forms.
fold in the walnuts, pumpkin seeds, and sesame seeds.
heat the ghee in a non-stick skillet over medium heat.
add the dough to the skillet and flatten it slightly with a spatula.
cook for 2-3 minutes on each side or until the bananas are caramelized and the dough is golden brown.
transfer the bale to a plate and serve warm with additional ghee if desired.
